title: Caption Prefix Vocabulary
description: Canonical caption-prefix vocabulary used across every AIG-RLIC+ portal page. Per APP-CC1 (owner: Ace, per META-CF; tone reviewed by Ray), every caption on every page MUST begin with one of the four canonical prefixes enumerated here. Caption body wording remains the author's discretion; the prefix is fixed. Closes the Wave-5 reproducibility-audit finding that caption wording (e.g. 'Takeaway:' vs 'What this shows:' vs 'In plain English:' vs 'Read this chart as:') varied arbitrarily across pages and across pairs. Referenced by APP-SE5 (Universal Takeaway Caption), APP-AF3 (Metric Interpretation), RES-2 (Translation Bridge), and the Evidence 8-element template's 'observation' element.

x-version.description: Semver of the vocabulary instance (separate from this schema's x-version). Bumped per META-CF whenever a prefix is added, renamed, or its semantic scope changes. Prefix REMOVAL is a breaking major bump.

x-owner.description: Single agent accountable for the vocabulary instance. Must be 'ace'. Ray reviews tone at handoff per RES-1 / RES-2 / META-ELI5.

x-generated.description: ISO-8601 date the instance was last regenerated.

notes.description: Freeform top-level notes about the instance (what was added in this version, migration notes for prefix renames).

prefixes.description: Map of canonical prefix string -> prefix definition. Prefix keys are the exact leading string rendered in st.caption / st.markdown (including the trailing colon and space).

prefix_definition.description: One canonical prefix plus metadata describing when to use it.

role.description: Short role tag describing the semantic function of the prefix.

when_to_use.description: One-sentence guidance on when a caption should use this prefix. Disambiguates from the other three.

example.description: One concrete example caption (prefix + body) drawn from or suitable for the HY-IG v2 reference pair.

sop_rule_ids.description: APP / RES rule IDs that use this prefix (e.g., APP-SE5 for 'Why this matters:', RES-2 for 'How to read it:' on translation bridges).
